title: Using Pronouns in Sentences
description: Practice pronouns and possessives in everyday sentences and dialogues

exercises.prompt: Choose the correct possessive: 'To je ___ pes.' (my dog, masculine)

data.question_cz: Vyberte správné přivlastňovací zájmeno: 'To je ___ pes.'
data.question_en: Choose the correct possessive: 'To je ___ pes.' (my dog, masculine)
data.explanation: pes is a masculine noun, so we use můj (my): To je můj pes. = This is my dog.

exercises.prompt: Translate to Czech: 'This is my car.'

data.grammar_note: auto is neuter, so we use moje (my): To je moje auto. The form mé is a shorter literary variant of moje, but moje is the standard spoken form.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank: 'Je to ___ kniha?' (Is this your book? — singular, feminine)

data.explanation: kniha is feminine, so we use tvoje (your, singular) or vaše (your, plural/formal). The informal singular form is tvoje: Je to tvoje kniha? = Is this your book?

exercises.prompt: Arrange the words to form a question: 'Is this your dog?'

data.explanation: Je to tvůj pes? = Is this your dog? pes is masculine, so we use tvůj (your, singular). The question word order inverts: verb 'Je' comes first.

exercises.prompt: Which two forms are both correct Czech for 'I am from Prague'?

data.question_cz: Které dvě věty jsou obě správné pro 'Jsem z Prahy'?
data.question_en: Which two forms are both correct Czech for 'I am from Prague'?
options: 'Jsem z Prahy.' and 'Já jsem z Prahy.'
data.explanation: Both 'Jsem z Prahy.' and 'Já jsem z Prahy.' are correct. The pronoun 'já' (I) is optional. Dropping it ('Jsem z Prahy.') is the default, natural form. Including it ('Já jsem z Prahy.') adds emphasis.

exercises.answer_key: 'Jsem z Prahy.' and 'Já jsem z Prahy.'

data.grammar_note: ona = she. učitelka = teacher (feminine). The pronoun ona can be dropped: 'Je učitelka.' also means 'She is a teacher.'

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank with the correct pronoun: '___ jsou z Prahy.' (They are from Prague.)

data.explanation: Oni (they, for masculine animate / mixed groups) or Ony (they, for feminine or inanimate groups). Oni jsou z Prahy. = They are from Prague. The pronoun can also be dropped: 'Jsou z Prahy.'

exercises.prompt: Translate to Czech: 'He is a student.'

data.grammar_note: On = he, student = student (masculine). Both 'On je student.' and 'Je student.' are correct — the pronoun is optional. Dropping it is more common in everyday speech.

exercises.prompt: Listen and write what you hear.

data.note: kniha is feminine, so we use moje (my): To je moje kniha. = This is my book.

data.target_text: To je můj pes. To je moje kniha. To je moje auto.
data.translation_en: This is my dog. This is my book. This is my car.

exercises.answer_key: To je můj pes. To je moje kniha. To je moje auto.

exercises.prompt: Complete the dialogue: You meet someone at a party and they ask about your belongings.

data.scenario: Meeting someone at a party — asking about belongings
data.explanation: In this dialogue, person_b answers possessive questions. 'Je to tvoje kniha?' → 'Ano, to je moje kniha.' (Yes, it's my book — feminine form). 'Je to tvůj pes?' → 'Ne, to není můj pes.' (No, it's not my dog — masculine form).
blank_answers: Ne, to nie je můj pes.

exercises.answer_key: Ano, to je moje kniha. Ne, to není můj pes.

exercises.prompt: Arrange the words to form: 'They are from Prague.'

data.explanation: Oni jsou z Prahy. = They are from Prague. Oni = they (masculine animate/mixed group). The pronoun can be dropped: 'Jsou z Prahy.' is equally correct and more common.
